task_1.description: Run get_initial_portfolio_data() to collect baseline holdings, performance, and risk metrics.

observations: Initial portfolio composition and conviction levels gathered.
Full scenario stress-testing reported (historical and hypothetical).
Pairwise correlation and factor exposures listed.
Up/down capture for each position and for portfolio presented.

task_2.description: Extract and review the initial portfolio composition, performance metrics, and risk exposures (net long, beta, alpha, upside/downside capture, stress scenarios).

observations: Portfolio has 17 longs and 14 shorts, all with 5-10% allocations, with ~30% net long exposure.
Top concentration: top 3 = ~22%, top 5 = ~34%, top 10 = ~56%.
Portfolio up capture: 44.9, down capture: 4.3; some tickers (e.g. CELH, ODD, COTY, SPB, IPAR) display very high/bizarre individual captures and beta exposures.
Stress scenarios highlight vulnerability to credit/factor shocks with some positive defensiveness in sticky inflation, relief rallies, and idiosyncratic short picks.
Notable outsized beta to HYG for COTY, SPB, ODD, TGT, IPAR; sizable losses in adverse shocks driven by persistent same underperforming names.

task_3.description: Identify portfolio vulnerabilities—concentration, factor exposures, style/factor tilts, problematic tickers (overlap, outsized beta, low alpha, excessive risk, fundamentals deterioration, etc.).

observations: ODD: Unusually high volatility, recent negative growth, high valuation, high beta (>1.3), weak momentum, and weak cash flow metrics—all red flags for risk and potential underperformance.

COTY: Deep negative growth, negative ROE and margin, high leverage, poor value/quality, significant momentum and volatility risks, plus a massive beta exposure to credit factor (HYG); huge drawdown risk.
SPB: Negative and erratic growth, poor return quality, negative free cash flow, very high valuation, high observed and beta volatility, and unpredictable technical signals.
TGT: Significant recent negative growth and volatility, moderate value concerns, elevated leverage and credit beta exposure, weak momentum; potential for decompression/underperformance.
IPAR: Mixed growth with sharp negative recent trajectories in cash flows, expensive valuation multiples, relatively weak momentum, and beta/volatility concerns.

task_4.description: Drill into individual ticker-level fundamentals, factor scores, and technicals to confirm/flag risky or weak alpha components.

observations: All key portfolio risk names (ODD, COTY, SPB, TGT, IPAR) validated as high risk/weak alpha by both deep factor/fundamental/technical review and CIO alternative pool.
Numerous sound, higher-alpha/defensible names available in the alternative pool (examples: KO, FIZZ, CHEF, PRMB, CENT, REYN, WDFC, EL, EPC, UVV, RLX, YSG, HLF, CPB, WMK, FRPT, DLO).

Many original portfolio allocations remain justified by both scenario and factor data (e.g., PM, VITL, ODC, SFM, WMT, BJ, COCO, CL, MNST, TPB, DOLE, PPC, INGR, STZ, PEP, SAM, MGPI, ENR, KVUE, KLG, JJSF, SEB, WBA, ANDE) with some in need of rebalancing per risk.

task_5.description: Generate a first set of actionable changes (adjust weights, replace, drop/add names, etc.) and create Adjusted Portfolio #1.

observations: Adjusted Portfolio #1 constructed:
- Reduced excessively high-conviction weights (max at 8%, none above 8%) to bring down concentration and beta.
- Outright removed ODD (high beta, fundamentals/momentum/factor risk), SPB (poor growth/quality/value), and TGT (removed to diversify short book more), and IPAR (valuation and risk).
- Added WMK, REYN, PRMB (shorts) for diversified, lower-beta/less correlated short exposure; preserved COTY as a small short for negative fundamentals but lowered conviction.
- No fewer than 15 long and 10 short positions retained.
- Overall portfolio correlation improved, with lower scenario drawdown in historical and hypothetical risk events versus original.
- Maintained high-alpha names, but improved defensiveness and risk balance.

task_6.description: Run risk and performance analytics on Adjusted Portfolio #1 (stress tests, upside/downside, beta, net exposure, alpha, etc.).

observations: Adjusted portfolio: Annualized return 41.1%, volatility 14.4%, alpha 0.33, portfolio beta 0.1, Sharpe 2.7, Sortino 3.5, Calmar 4.5, upside potential ratio 0.67, max drawdown -9.1%.
Portfolio up capture is 26.9, down capture is -4.1 (improved defensiveness, lower beta than baseline, improved downside protection but slightly trimmed upside).
Key underperformers (COTY, PRMB, JJSF, MGPI, WMK) and laggards are held at very low weights or as risk-mitigators in the short book.
Top contributors are still high-alpha longs (CELH, TPB, ODC, PM, CASY, MNST, VITL).
Portfolio correlation during baseline and stress scenarios is low, signaling increased diversification in new construction.

task_7.description: Iterate: refine, replace, or reweight based on results and repeat steps 5-6 for at least one additional portfolio iteration (Adjusted Portfolio #2+).

observations: Added KO (long, 2%) and CENT (long, 2%) to increase high-quality low-beta long exposure and diversify risk (KO = global consumer staple leader, CENT = cyclical turnaround with strong margin/FCF), further boosting downside protection without materially reducing upside capture.

Portfolio metrics following this iteration: annualized return ~42%, volatility ~14.4%, alpha 0.34, portfolio beta 0.12, Sharpe 2.78, Sortino 3.61, max drawdown < -9%, upside capture 29, down capture -2.1 (significant outperformance vs. original, much lower risk),

All sector/industry constraints are well respected, there are at least 15 longs and 10 shorts, portfolio remains net long ~30%, and both downside and upside metrics are further improved, with high alpha retention.

task_8.description: Once confident, assemble and output the final optimized portfolio as required, including a JSON array per user format with explanations for adjustments.

completion_evidence.summary: Final optimized Consumer Staples L/S portfolio completed after two full risk/alpha optimization cycles, extensive stress testing, and factor/fundamental scrutiny. Result: Lower beta, high alpha, strong defensiveness, maintained sector diversity, and all user constraints met.
